entries.description: Routed session-verification via gpt-5-4 (round 1: ISSUES_FOUND, round 2: VERIFIED).
Round 1 raised 1 Major + 1 Minor; both fixed and re-verified clean in round 2. issues-001.json saved.
Routed session-verification via gpt-5-4 (R1: ISSUES_FOUND with 2 Major; R2: VERIFIED after fixes).

Round 1 raised 2 Major (PyPI workflow tag-glob would also match vsix-v* tags; CHANGELOG over-claimed Marketplace publication had landed). Both fixed: added '!vsix-v*' negative pattern to release.yml's on.push.tags, revised runbook's tag-coexistence section, reworded CHANGELOG [0.13.0] Added block to 'Marketplace-publish-ready' framing. Round 2 returned VERIFIED.

Routed session-verification via gpt-5-4 (R1: ISSUES_FOUND with 3 Minor findings re verbatim-move calibration; R2: ISSUES_FOUND with 1 wording-precision nit on the change-log; nit addressed inline without a Round 3 per the verifier's stated remediation path).

Round 1 raised 3 Minor about the spec's 'verbatim move' language: (1) state-derivation-table merge in the deep-dive section, (2) six new file-map rows beyond the verbatim move, (3) Set 011 prerequisite edit too broad. Issue 3 fixed by tightening the prerequisite line; issues 1 and 2 authorized via Set 012's change-log per the verifier's stated 'amend the change-log' remediation. Round 2 cleared issues 1+3, raised one wording-precision nit on the change-log's path enumeration; addressed inline by clarifying the docs/planning/ prefix on runbook entries and adding the 'all other pre-existing rows carried over unchanged' assertion. No Round 3 — finding was documentation-quality not correctness.
